The next morning, Sabrina was sitting on a bench outside, waiting for Ben to show up. She was dressed in a pair of khaki pants, a plain black hoodie, and black tennis shoes. When she finally saw Ben approaching her, she rolled her eyes.

Ben was wearing a tight turquoise sweater, a white pleated skirt, and the same pink flats she had worn last night. It didn’t go together, but she figured he tried his best. What he didn’t try to do at all, though, was makeup. He had clearly gone to sleep without removing any of the makeup he already had on, and hadn’t bothered trying to fix it in the morning. That said, it looked fine.

“The skirt was a brave choice,” Sabrina remarked when Ben sat down. “Close your legs when you sit, please.”

Ben did as he was told. “Well, I wasn’t going to put on another pair of your tight-ass pants. And a skirt seemed like a better choice than a dress.”

“That’s fair,” Sabrina said. When she looked at Ben again, she noticed something else. “Ben, are you wearing a bra?”

“No,” Ben said, avoiding eye contact. “Too much trouble.”

“Oh my god. I’m going to look like a total slut when we switch back,” Sabrina sighed. “What did Maggie say when you left looking like that?”

“Nothing. I left while she was in the shower. We didn’t really talk last night either. She was too tired. What about you and Eric? Anything I should know?”

Sabrina hesitated. Should she tell Ben about her awkward encounter with Eric last night? He wouldn’t be happy about it.
142122.title: Go to bed and forget it

142123.content:
Sabrina let out a heavy sigh. “Just forget it. Forget this ever happened.”

“Gladly,” said Eric, closing his eyes and letting his head hit the pillow.

Sabrina turned the light off and fell asleep, still wearing Ben’s sweater and jeans.

____________________

The next morning, Sabrina was sitting on a bench outside, waiting for Ben to show up. She was dressed in a pair of khaki pants, a plain black hoodie, and black tennis shoes. When she finally saw Ben approaching her, she rolled her eyes.

Ben was wearing a tight turquoise sweater, a white pleated skirt, and the same pink flats she had worn last night. It didn’t go together, but she figured he tried his best. What he didn’t try to do at all, though, was makeup. He had clearly gone to sleep without removing any of the makeup he already had on, and hadn’t bothered trying to fix it in the morning. That said, it looked fine.

“The skirt was a brave choice,” Sabrina remarked when Ben sat down. “Close your legs when you sit, please.”

Ben did as he was told. “Well, I wasn’t going to put on another pair of your tight-ass pants. And a skirt seemed like a better choice than a dress.”

“That’s fair,” Sabrina said. When she looked at Ben again, she noticed something else. “Ben, are you wearing a bra?”

“No,” Ben said, avoiding eye contact. “Too much trouble.”

“Oh my god. I’m going to look like a total slut when we switch back,” Sabrina sighed. “What did Maggie say when you left looking like that?”

“Nothing. I left while she was in the shower. We didn’t really talk last night either. She was too tired. What about you and Eric? Anything I should know?”

Sabrina hesitated. Should she tell Ben about her awkward encounter with Eric last night? He wouldn’t be happy about it.
142123.title: Go to bed and forget it

1422.content:
Sabrina headed off to catch up with Eric, leaving Ben to head upstairs. Once Ben made it to the girls’ dorm room, he saw that Maggie was waiting for him. Well, waiting for Sabrina, which is who she thought Ben was.

“Hey, Sabrina,” Maggie said in a more shy tone than she normally used. “I want to tell you something.”

“Um, okay. Sure. You can tell me anything. I’m your best friend after all. I’m Sabrina. Your friend. That’s me,” Ben rambled, trying his best not to sound stupid.

“Well... okay, here goes...” Maggie started. “I know that you kind of have a thing for Ben, and I don’t want to make things weird, but I have to be honest, and, well... oh, I should just say it. I have a crush on _____.”
1422.title: Ben

14221.content:
“Wait, what?” Ben asked, taken aback.

“I like him. And I know you do too, but let’s face it, you haven’t made a move on him yet. So I wanted to know... how would you feel if I asked him out?”

Ben was speechless. He had no idea that either girl liked him. He thought they were both great, but now he found himself in an unexpected scenario.

“I understand if you don’t know what to say,” said Maggie. “And it’s okay if you’re angry with me. I really want to ask him out, but technically you liked him first. So if you don’t want me to ask him out, please tell me.”

“Actually,” Ben said...
14221.title: “Ben”

142211.content:
“Go for it,” Ben said, smiling.

“Wait. Really?”

“Yeah.”

“You’re not mad?”

“It’s like you said. I had my chance, and I haven’t made a move. Maybe we weren’t meant to be. It’s all you now... girl,” Ben said, trying his best to talk the way Sabrina would.

“Okay! Oh my gosh! Okay! I’ll talk to him first thing in the morning! Thank you so much! You’re the best!” Maggie said, hugging Ben tightly.

Ben was a little worried now. If Maggie asked him out, he would definitely say yes, but what would Sabrina say? She was currently living his life after all. Ben decided that in the morning, he should...
142211.title: “Go for it.”

1422111.content:
Saturday morning, Maggie and Ben both took showers, then prepared to head out.

Maggie got dressed in a yellow romper and the same high heeled wedges from last night. She fixed her makeup, put in her nose ring, and tied her hair back in a high ponytail.

Ben opted for a university hoodie, yoga pants, and a pair of white sandals. He didn’t bother worrying with makeup or a bra, because he didn’t plan on being Sabrina for much longer.

“Alright,” said Maggie, “I’ll text Ben and tell him that I want to have breakfast with him. If he says yes, I’ll meet him downstairs and we’ll leave together. Then while we’re out, I’ll ask him if he wants to go on a real date tonight.”

“Sounds good. I’ll come downstairs with you so I can... do a thing,” Ben said, grabbing the remote and not even trying to formulate an excuse.

Maggie, in the process of texting who she thought was Ben, was too anxious to care what the real Ben said. “Sure, sure. Oh my god, he said yes to breakfast! Okay, let’s go.”

Once downstairs, Ben saw that Sabrina also hadn’t bothered trying to dress nice. She wore a plain orange hoodie with blue jeans and the same white sneakers from last night.

“Ready to go, Ben?” Maggie asked Sabrina.

“Yeah,” she answered. “Will Sabrina be joining us?”

“No, I’m just here... to say... have fun, or something,” Ben said, aiming the remote at Sabrina and taking his life back.

Ben, now back to normal, began to leave with Maggie.

“Oh, Ben, I just remembered,” Sabrina said, stopping them. “You and me were gonna talk about... that project. You know the one.”

“Well, we can do that later,” Ben said, eagerly awaiting his day with Maggie.

Sabrina frowned. When she had accepted Maggie’s breakfast invite, she hadn’t known what to expect. She didn’t think she liked how the morning was going so far. Now Ben and Maggie were on their way out, and Ben...
1422111.title: Take his life back

14221111.content:
“Where are we headed?” Ben asked, climbing into the passenger seat of Maggie’s pristinely restored antique Volkswagen Beetle.

“That depends,” said Maggie. “What do you want for breakfast?”

“Well, I could go for some pancakes. I guess we could go to Frankie’s.”

“Frankie’s? Please,” Maggie scoffed, putting the car in gear. “I’m taking you to Katrina’s Kitchen. Katy has the best pancake recipe.”

“Works for me,” Ben said, settling in.

Once they arrived at their destination, Maggie led Ben inside and signaled the host.

“Miss Chan!” the host greeted her. “Good morning! Two?”

“Two,” Maggie confirmed.

The host then led them to a table, and handed them their menus. “Your server will be with you shortly,” he said.

“Thank you!” Maggie said cheerfully.

“This place is a little fancy for breakfast, don’t you think?” Ben stated.

“It’s totally worth it. You’ll see,” said Maggie. “And don’t worry about money. Order whatever you want.”

“I take it you come here often?” Ben guessed.

“I’ve been coming here since I was little,” Maggie explained. “Katrina and my mom are really good friends.”

The two were then approached by a young waitress. She looked like she was probably sixteen or seventeen. She had her blonde hair tied back in a single braid, and she wasn’t terribly tall. Maggie didn’t recognize her, and figured she must be new.

Maggie ordered water for herself and a soda for Ben, then ordered two pancake specials, requesting that Katrina herself make them if she wasn’t too busy. Maggie and Ben then continued to chat until the waitress returned with their meals.

“Your name is Maggie, right?” the girl asked. “Katrina says hi. Enjoy!”

“So, Ben,” Maggie said nervously, watching her friend eat. “I have to tell you something. I invited you out this morning because I want to ask you a question.”

“Go for it,” Ben said between bites, knowing exactly where this was going.

“Would you be interested in going on a date with me? Tonight, that is.”

“I would love to,” Ben answered without hesitating.

Maggie blinked. “Well that was easy,” she said.

Ben smiled. “Did you have anything in mind?”

“I was thinking we could...”
14221111.title: Kept the remote

142211111.content:
“That sounds good,” said Ben. “In fact, we could hang out all day if you want. There’s no reason to waste gas driving all the way across town just to come back here later.”

“Don’t you and Sabrina have some project you need to work on?” Maggie asked, recalling their interaction in the hallway earlier.

“Oh yeah,” said Ben, remembering the remote. “Well...”

“Well what?”

Ben wondered if he really wanted to talk to Sabrina right now. He was having a perfectly good time with Maggie, and he wanted it to continue.

Making a decision, Ben got the remote out of his pocket. “Maggie, I have something I want to show you.”

“This isn’t gonna get weird, is it?” Maggie asked. “I’d rather not have this relationship ruined before it even starts.”

“No, it’s gonna be cool. Trust me,” said Ben, adjusting the settings so Maggie would see what happened, then swapping himself with...
142211111.title: “...come back here for dinner.”

142211112.content:
“We just saw a movie last night,” said Ben.

“I meant a play,” Maggie clarified. “The university is doing Hairspray. Tonight is opening night. I mean, unless you’re not into musicals.”

“Sounds fun,” said Ben, finishing his food. “You know, this might be the best pancake I’ve ever eaten.”

“Dude, I told you,” Maggie laughed. “So, tonight?”

“It’s a date,” said Ben. “What time does the play start?”

“Eight, I think. You can dress up if you want to. I have a new dress I’ve been wanting to wear.”

“Awesome.”

Ben waited for Maggie to finish eating, then she paid the check, and they hopped in her car and headed back to the dorms. When Ben arrived back in his room, he found Sabrina waiting for him.

“Uh, hi?” he said.

“Eric told me I could wait here for you,” Sabrina explained. “He went to the gym.”

“Gotcha,” said Ben. “What’s up?”

“What do you think? We said we were going to talk about the remote.”

“I know, I know. But something came up.”

“What? Your little breakfast date with my roommate?”

“It was just a regular breakfast,” Ben defended himself. “Our... date is actually tonight.”

Sabrina opened her mouth to respond, but paused. She eventually managed to let out a simple, “Oh.”

“We can test the remote now, but I need to be dressed up in time to meet Maggie before eight.”

“Did... did you ask her out?” Sabrina asked.

Ben suddenly remembered what Maggie had said last night. Sabrina liked Ben too. And Maggie had confessed to Ben when he was in Sabrina’s life, so the real Sabrina had no knowledge of Maggie’s crush. And Ben, acting selfishly, had taken advantage of the situation to make Maggie think that she had Sabrina’s blessing. “Actually, she asked me out,” Ben admitted.

“Wow. I had no idea that she liked you,” Sabrina said, trying not to show any emotion. She then reached for the door. “I think I need to go talk to her.”

Ben panicked. If he allowed Sabrina to talk to Maggie, then surely she would find out what Ben had done. Ben didn’t want Sabrina to be angry with him or Maggie. He had to think of something quickly.
142211112.title: “...go see a show.”

choices: Try to have a normal date
1422111121.content:
Without thinking, Ben fired the remote at Sabrina, switching lives with her again, and now standing in her way.

“Ben, what are you doing?” Sabrina asked.

“I don’t think you should talk to Maggie.”

“Why not?”

“Because...” Ben sighed. “Because last night, when she thought I was you, she kind of asked for your permission to ask me out.”

“Oh,” Sabrina said again. “And, you told her yes, apparently.”

“What was I supposed to say?”

“I... don’t know,” Sabrina said, avoiding eye contact. “So, I guess that means you know that I...”

“Yeah, I know,” Ben admitted.

“And... you chose her.”

“Sabrina...”

“It’s okay,” Sabrina sighed. “I guess I should have made my move sooner. Can you switch us back please?”

Ben did as she asked, then watched as she turned to leave.

“Sabrina,” he tried again.

“Please don’t,” she said. “I hope you two have a nice time tonight. I need to be alone right now.”

Ben was conflicted. On one hand, he felt bad for Sabrina. He couldn’t imagine how she felt right now. On the other hand, he was genuinely excited to go out with Maggie.

Looking at the remote, Ben wondered if he could use it to somehow fix this mess. Then again, maybe using the remote would only cause more trouble. Maybe the best thing to do was to wait until tonight and try to have a normal date with Maggie.
1422111121.title: Stop her

choices: Resist temptation, and get rid of the remote
Give in to temptation, and take Maggie’s life
1422111122.content:
Sabrina wasn’t sure what to think. As she made her way up the stairs, she contemplated chewing out Maggie for asking Ben out when she knew perfectly well that Sabrina liked him.

When she got in the room, she found Maggie in a very good mood. She was lying down on her bed, AirPods in her ears, and hands behind her head.

“Sabrina!” Maggie said, sitting up and taking her earbuds out. “Thank you so much.”

“For what?” Sabrina asked.

“For letting me ask Ben out. I would never have done it without your permission.”

Sabrina’s eyes widened as she realized what had happened. It was all Ben’s doing. “Yeah, I’m... happy for you,” she said, no longer upset with Maggie. Ben, though... Ben was in big trouble.

Without saying another word, Sabrina left Maggie alone and headed right back downstairs to Ben’s room, entering without knocking.

“Benjamin, explain yourself,” she demanded.

“Explain myself,” Ben echoed. “I have a feeling you’ve already figured it out.”

“You’re damn right I figured it out,” she said, slamming the door shut. “Ben, how could you?”

“What was I supposed to do? Maggie said that she liked me. I didn’t want to hurt her feelings. I mean, what would you have done?”

“I don’t know,” Sabrina admitted. “But... I don’t think I want to talk to either one of you for a while. You’ve clearly chosen her over me, and she has it in her mind that I’m over you.”

“Sabrina, what do you want me to say?”

Sabrina thought for a moment. “Give me the remote,” she said.

“What?”

“Give me the remote. I’m gonna get rid of it so neither of us can use it to cause any more drama.”

“Sabrina...”

“I’m serious.”

Ben did as he was told, handing the device over and trying not to look Sabrina in the eye.

“Maybe we’ll forget that this swapping stuff ever happened,” Sabrina said, leaving the room.

As she walked down the hall, she studied the device. Was getting rid of it really the best way to proceed? Or could she get away with using it herself? Maybe she could borrow Maggie’s life for a while. That way, she would be the one going on a date with Ben. After all, if Ben could be selfish, why couldn’t she?
1422111122.title: Let her go

14221111221.content:
Sabrina walked outside into the parking lot, not wanting to believe everything that had happened since last night. In time, she might be able to forgive Maggie. None of this was her fault. But Ben?

Approaching her car Sabrina thought about what she was doing. She couldn’t just take the remote back to where they had found it, but she didn’t want to throw it away either. Buttons could get pushed by accident if she did that. And either way, she risked someone else finding it and causing trouble of their own.

No, she thought. The best thing to do would be to destroy it.

Sabrina placed the remote on the ground under her car, betting that it would break if she ran it over. Then, she got in and started the engine. But as she reached for the gearshift, she had one last moment of hesitation.
14221111221.title: Resist temptation, and get rid of the remote

142211112212.content:
Sabrina took her hand off of the gearshift and sighed. She just couldn’t do it. Maybe she thought destroying the remote could be dangerous. Maybe she didn’t want to see such an incredible scientific invention be lost to the world. She couldn’t say for sure.

Sabrina got out, picked the remote up off of the ground, then sat back down in the car, wondering what to do. She was definitely not going to give it back to Ben, but she didn’t want to keep it for herself either.

Figuring she could make a final decision at a later time, Sabrina put the remote in her glove compartment, then put the car in gear and decided to just drive around for a while.

——————————

A FEW MONTHS LATER

“Well, we’ve been in every room in the house,” said Sabrina. “It’s not here.”

“Did you try calling it?” asked Grace.

“Yeah. Three times,” said Sabrina. “I know you keep saying you didn’t leave it in my car, but-”

“I didn’t,” Grace insisted.

“Can we check anyway?”

“Fine.”

The two sisters made their way out into the garage. Sabrina unlocked her car, then opened the driver’s side door and got in. Grace got in on the other side, and both girls began to search in every nook and cranny of the vehicle.

After finding all of the cupholders empty, Grace decided to look in the glove box. Opening it up and peering inside, she spotted something hiding in the back. Could that be it? Moving her sister’s junk out of the way, Grace reached into the glove box for whatever it was.

“Found it,” said Sabrina, holding up her sister’s cell phone, which had fallen under the driver’s seat.

“Wait, then what’s this?” Grace asked, pulling out what she had spotted.

“Oh my god, I forgot that was there,” said Sabrina.

“Well... what is it?” Grace asked, preparing to push a button.

“Wait, Grace, don’t!”

*FLASH*
142211112212.title: Don’t do it

1422111122121.content:
“What the fuck? Sabrina,” said Grace, “you... you look just like me!”

“Grace, I think you should look in the mirror,” Sabrina said, sighing and blowing a strand of hair out of her face.

Reaching up and adjusting the rearview mirror, Grace took a look at her reflection. Instead of her own face looking back at her, it was Sabrina’s.

“What the... I’m... I’m you? What’s happening?”

“Grace, calm down,” said Sabrina, snatching the remote from her sister. “You’re still you. You just have my body.”

“W...what?”

“We’re still in the same seats, which means we didn’t switch lives,” Sabrina explained. “Like I said, you’re still you. You just look like me.”

“But... how?”

“How do you think?” asked Sabrina, waving the remote in front of her face.

“That thing? Why was that in your car?” Grace asked. “Where did it come from?”

“It’s... we found it.”

“We?”

“One of my friends. A guy that I liked. He found it a few months ago, then he did something that made me mad, so I took it.”

“And... it’s been in your car all this time?”

“Apparently,” said Sabrina. “I guess I just put it out of my mind. Now hold still. I’m going to switch us back.”
1422111122121.title: Body swap

choices: Swap back and give Grace the remote
Swap back and keep the remote
14221111221212.content:
“Hold on a minute,” said Grace. “Can... can we stay like this for a little bit?”

“You’ve got to be joking,” said Sabrina.

“You said it’s just our bodies, and I’m still me, right? What’s the hurry to switch back?”

“Grace, please. Playing around with this thing has already led to drama once.”

“Sabrina, we just switched bodies! You understand that, right?” Grace appealed. “This is like something out of a movie, and you want to just switch back and have me forget about it?”

Sabrina let out a long, tired sigh. “How long do you want to keep my body?”

“I don’t know. Like, a day?”

“I... I just don’t know that it’s a good idea,” Sabrina hesitated.

“Well, how about this?” Grace offered. “You can switch us back, but I want the remote.”

“Now I know you’re joking. There’s no way that’s happening.”

“Come on! Be a fun person for once,” Grace whined.

I’m fun, thought Sabrina. Am I not fun?

Frowning, Sabrina made her decision.
14221111221212.title: Grace stops Sabrina

142211112212123.content:
Giving her sister a scolding look, Sabrina activated the remote and switched their bodies back, then made a point of putting the device in her own pocket.

“Boooooriiiiiiiing,” Grace whined, back in her own skin.

“I’m not boring. I’m practical,” Sabrina insisted, huffing and going back in the house.

“You are too, you boring bitch,” Grace muttered under her breath, following her sister inside.

The minutes went by, and eventually the girls’ mother Lynne got home. The family chatted, had dinner, hung out some more, then went to bed.

In the morning, Sabrina awoke and got ready for work. She and Grace worked together at the same store in the summer, and since Grace didn’t have a car, Sabrina usually drove her. Sabrina liked getting to work early, which meant she was often ready to go well before Grace was. Today was one of those days.

“Grace,” Sabrina called out, knocking on her sister’s bedroom door. “Are you riding with me?”

“I’ll ride with Mom,” Grace answered.

Actually, Grace was considering not going to work at all today. She suspected that Sabrina had stashed the remote somewhere in the house, and she planned on finding it. If she found it quickly, then sure, she would let Lynne drive her to work. And if she didn’t find it at all, maybe she would end up biking to work and facing the consequences for being late.

Sabrina was suspicious whenever Grace didn’t ride with her. Grace was no stranger to faking sick or just not caring. She was 15 after all. But before the summer ended, she would be 16, and she would have her own car. Then Sabrina would have one less thing to worry about.

The remote, of course, was hidden. Sabrina thought Grace might try to take it, so Sabrina had decided to...
142211112212123.title: Swap back and keep the remote

1422111122121231.content:
Grace watched out her bedroom window as her sister drove away. As soon as Sabrina’s car was out of sight, Grace quickly snuck into Sabrina’s room. She was careful not to be too loud, as she didn’t want her mother to ask what she was doing.

“Grace, honey,” Lynne called. “Are you getting ready?”

“Yeah mom,” Grace yelled back as she rifled through Sabrina’s things. She methodically made her way though the whole room. Drawers, shelves, the closet, under the bed... nothing.

Then, Grace remembered something. When the girls were younger, Sabrina had a diary. A small, pocket sized diary that she kept in a lockbox. Grace, of course, knew the code to the lockbox. She had been a nosy child, and she had read some of Sabrina’s diary, but she found it boring. Sabrina had never been the wiser, and to this day believed that the contents of her old diary were a secret. Now, Grace was curious if the lockbox was still in it’s old hiding place.

“Two minutes, Grace!” Lynne called out again.

“Okayyyy,” Grace replied, opening the bottom drawer on Sabrina’s vanity, then carefully taking the drawer all the way out. “Nice,” she said, spotting the lockbox neatly tucked into the hollow spot beneath where the drawer fit in.

Grinning from ear to ear, Grace took out the lockbox and put in the code she remembered, hoping that Sabrina hadn’t changed it since they were little. Upon hearing the mechanism unlock, Grace’s grin grew even bigger. Of course it worked. Boring, predictable Sabrina.

Grace opened the lockbox to see a few things. The old diary, for one. A love note from an early crush, some emergency cash, and... ah, yes. There it was. The remote.

“Grace, I’m leaving!” Lynne called out a final time. “Come now, or you’re on your own!”
1422111122121231.title: Hide it in the house

choices: Put the remote away for now
1422111122122.content:
Grace looked around, confused. Why was she suddenly sitting on the other side of the car and wearing her sister’s clothes?

“My phone!” said Sabrina, now in Grace’s place.

Sure enough, Grace was now holding her phone in her right hand. And weirdly enough, the remote she had found in the glove box was still in her left.

“Well, can I have it?” Sabrina demanded.

“What?”

“Can I have my phone, Sabrina?”

“But, you’re...”

“What? Are you waiting for me to say I was wrong?” Sabrina asked. “I left it in your car. You were right. Okay?”

Did... did Sabrina think that she was Grace? It sure seemed that way. Either that, or this was Sabrina’s weird idea of a joke.

“Y-yeah. Here,” Grace said, handing over her phone.

“Thanks. What’s that?”

“This?” Grace looked at the remote. “Um... nothing.”

“Okay,” said Sabrina, going back inside the house.

Grace stayed in the car, staring at the strange device in her hand.

This is crazy, she thought. This can’t be real. But, it worked. What do I do? Do I use it again? I can’t be Sabrina. Or... can I?
1422111122122.title: Life swap

14221111221221.content:
Examining the remote’s many features, Grace followed her sister into the house and to the living room.

Sabrina plopped herself onto Grace’s side of the couch and began scrolling through Grace’s phone, all the while acting like nothing was wrong. It was incredible.

Once Grace felt like she more or less understood the remote, she input her desired settings and activated it a second time,

*FLASH*

And voila. Grace now had Sabrina’s body to go along with her life.

Sabrina’s body was a little taller, a little blonder, and had a rounder face and bigger boobs. As they grew older, the two girls started to look more and more alike, but they still had distinctly different features. Grace had been told before that she looked a bit like her father, although she had never met him to confirm that. Sabrina, on the other hand, was the spitting image of their mother, Lynne.

Speaking of their mother, it was nearly six o’clock. Lynne would be getting home any minute now, and Grace was eager to test the remote with multiple subjects.

Deciding to get into character, Grace sat down on Sabrina’s side of the couch and closed her eyes. The minutes went by, and eventually, the girls heard the garage open, then close, followed by the sound of someone entering the house.

“Hi girls,” Lynne called out. “I see that the kitchen is clean.”

“I did that,” both girls responded, followed by Sabrina giving Grace a confused look.

“I, uh, I mean... Grace did it,” said Grace.

“Well, I appreciate it,” said Lynne, entering the living room and sighing as she sat down in her own chair. “My day was exhausting, but I won’t bore you two. What about your day? How was car shopping for the almost-sixteen-year-old?”

“Pretty good. We found a couple that I think I like, but nothing has really called my name yet,” said Sabrina.

As she listened to her mother and life-swapped sister talk, Grace eyed the remote again, deciding to swap something of Lynne’s with...
14221111221221.title: Try another swap

choices: Try to have a nice time
Try to sabotage Ben and Maggie’s relationship
14221111222.content:
By the time Sabrina made it back to her own room, she was out of breath. She had just used the stairs three times in a very short time period. But she didn’t sit down to take a breather. Instead, she aimed the device at Maggie and fired.

*FLASH*

Sabrina now found herself lying down on Maggie’s bed. She sat up, catching a glimpse of her reflection in the mirror across the room. She was dressed in Maggie’s yellow romper, and it had been resized to fit her.

Looking to the doorway, she saw Maggie dressed in her clothes.

“Hi, Sabrina,” she said to Maggie.

“Hi,” Maggie said, believing that she was indeed Sabrina.

“How do you feel?”

“I... think I just had a fight with Ben,” Maggie replied, grabbing Sabrina’s purse. “I think I need to be alone right now.”

“Do whatever you need to do,” Sabrina said, watching Maggie leave.

Sabrina felt a rush. What she was doing was evil, but she loved it. She looked down at the remote in her hand, and noticed that her fingernails were now painted blue. Grinning, Sabrina let herself fall back and enjoy Maggie’s music.

The hours flew by, and before Sabrina knew it, it was time for her date with Ben. She wondered how she should proceed.
14221111222.title: Give in to temptation, and take Maggie’s life

choices: Find another girl to mess with
14221112.content:
As Sabrina watched Ben and Maggie leave, she was overcome with suspicion. What is he not telling me? she wondered.

Whatever was happening with Maggie aside, Sabrina couldn’t help but feel like it was a good thing that Ben had left her alone with the remote. He probably would have wanted to use it for something perverted anyway.

As she studied the many buttons on the device, she wondered what to do first.
14221112.title: Gave the remote to Sabrina

1422112.content:
Saturday morning, Maggie and Ben both took showers, then prepared to head out.

Maggie got dressed in a yellow romper and the same high heeled wedges from last night. She fixed her makeup, put in her nose ring, and tied her hair back in a high ponytail.

Ben opted for a university hoodie, yoga pants, and a pair of white sandals. He didn’t bother worrying with makeup or a bra, even though he knew Sabrina wouldn’t like that.

“Alright,” said Maggie, “I’ll text Ben and tell him that I want to have breakfast with him. If he says yes, I’ll meet him downstairs and we’ll leave together. Then while we’re out, I’ll ask him if he wants to go on a date tonight.”

“Actually,” said Ben, trying to rush the process so he could see how Sabrina responded, “why don’t you just cut right to the chase? I’ll come downstairs with you, and I’ll stay out of sight. That way, if he says no, I can, you know, be there for you.”

“You think he’ll say no?” Maggie looked at Ben, worried.

“Uh, no. I think he would love to go out with you. He just might... be caught off guard, you know?”

“Well, okay. Here goes.”

The two headed downstairs. Ben hid around a corner, close enough to hear what was happening, and Maggie knocked on the boys’ door. Sabrina answered the door, dressed in a plain orange hoodie, blue jeans, and the same white sneakers from last night.

“Hi, Maggie. What’s up?”

“Okay, here goes,” Maggie said, taking a deep breath. “I was gonna invite you to breakfast, but I guess I should just cut to the chase. Ben, would you like to go on a date with me tonight?”

“I... uh... a date?” Sabrina stammered.

“Yeah, I was thinking dinner. I’ll pay for everything,” Maggie offered.

Sabrina’s mind raced. The real Ben probably wouldn’t hesitate to go out with a girl, especially someone as cool as Maggie. But she wasn’t sure how long she was going to be in Ben’s life, and she didn’t want to make anything awkward.

“Ben,” came Maggie again, “what do you say?”

“Hey, have you been working out already?” she asked him.

“Yeah. I jogged here,” Ben answered.

“You never jog,” Sabrina said, beginning to put the pieces together.

“I know! But for some reason, it’s what I wanted to do. And I think I know why. It’s the remote,” Ben said quietly, patting the bag on his back.

“You think it worked,” Sabrina inferred.

“I do,” said Ben, “I think we switched personalities, and I think we should switch back.”

“Well... what if I don’t want a girly personality?” Sabrina asked.

“Are you saying you don’t want to go back to normal?”

“I’m saying that this could be the new normal. I feel so relaxed. I like it.”

“Sabrina, don’t be ridiculous. I’m going to fix this before it gets too weird.”

With that, Ben took the remote out of his bag, and aimed it at Sabrina.
1532.title: The gym

15322.content:
“Dude, wait!” Sabrina said, pleading with Ben. “I just want to stay like this for a little while.”

Ben waited, curious to hear Sabrina make her case.

“Look, let’s finish our workout,” said Sabrina, “then we can go out and try some activities to test our personalities. We can switch back later.”

“What kind of activities?”

“Stuff that I like, stuff that you like, of course our interests will be switched. We can go shopping, since that’s something you’ll like now. And I can play some video games that I wouldn’t have liked before.”

Ben thought about it. “Fine. But we stay together all day. And we switch back before we go to bed.”

“We switch back if we don’t decide to stay this way,” said Sabrina.

“Whatever,” said Ben, making his way to the other side of the room to do some yoga poses.

Sabrina grinned, and got back on the treadmill, hoping that Ben would end up liking his new self enough to stay this way.

After the two finished working out, they headed to...
15322.title: She stops him

153221.content:
“Alright, girly boy,” Sabrina remarked once the two arrived at the mall, “I’m following your lead. For now.”

“I know exactly where to go first,” said Ben, walking with purpose and a feminine gait.

“I have a feeling I know where you’re taking us,” said Sabrina.

Sure enough, Sabrina’s personality compelled Ben to go to the largest clothing store in the mall. Ben’s face lit up like a child at Christmas, and he began looking at every price tag he could find.

“Um, Ben?” said Sabrina, taking note of the specific items Ben was admiring.

“Yes?” Ben answered, studying the outfit on a display mannequin.

“Do you, by chance, happen to notice a problem?”

“What problem?”

“You’re looking in the women’s section, dude,” Sabrina pointed out.

Ben blinked, then seemed embarrassed. “Ohmygod, you’re right. I guess this is just what I’m interested in now.”

“Understandable. These clothes aren’t suited for that body though.”

Ben thought for a moment. “Well, let’s change my body, then.”

“Wow. Okay. Big steps,” Sabrina said, unsure exactly how to respond.

“These outfits are to die for, Sabrina,” said Ben, getting the remote out. “I definitely want some of it, and I don’t want the world to view me as a crossdresser.”

“Alright then, whose body are you gonna steal?” Sabrina said, looking around.

There was a store employee nearby. She was a thin African-American woman with long hair combed behind her ears. She was dressed in a red skirt and blouse combo, with black heels, white dangling earrings, and a fancy name tag that read “Vivian”.

Ben looked at the woman, then at Sabrina, then at the remote. “If I’m gonna try clothes on, then I want to do it with...”
153221.title: The mall

choices: Keep his own clothes for now
1532211.content:
“Really?” asked Sabrina.

“Yes. Look at her. She’s flawless,” said Ben, handing the remote to Sabrina. “Here. You do it. Switch my body with hers.”

“Are you sure it will work?” Sabrina asked, eyeing the remote’s various buttons.

“The personality function clearly worked.”

“Well, yeah,” Sabrina nodded as she adjusted the settings. “You ready?”

“Yes.”

*FLASH*

“Holy shit! It worked!” Sabrina exclaimed, looking at the black woman’s body now dressed in Ben’s clothes.

“Hellz yeah it did!” Ben said, smiling from ear to ear with his new face. “Oh my god, I feel so sexy and mature.”

“Do you want her clothes too?” Sabrina asked, watching the woman walk around the store with Ben’s body, looking silly in her red blouse and skirt.

“I want these clothes,” said Ben, continuing to eye price tags.

“No, I mean... you mentioned being a crossdresser a second ago,” Sabrina explained. “But, no matter what body you have, you’re still dressed like a guy right now. If you keep your own clothes, then people might think it’s weird if you start trying on stuff from this store. But if you swap clothes with that woman, then no one will think twice about you trying on this stuff, because it would be how you already dress.”

“I understand what you’re saying,” said Ben. “But I don’t think we should make too many swaps at once. I’m already a little worried that I won’t get my body back.”

“Okay, well... what if you take my clothes instead?” Sabrina offered. “With our personalities swapped, it would probably make both of us more comfortable anyway.”

Ben thought about his options, then decided that he wanted to...
1532211.title: “That employee’s body.”

1532212.content:
“Sounds good to me,” said Sabrina.

“Okay, let me see,” said Ben, changing the remote’s settings. “Here we go...”

*FLASH*

“Dude, it worked!” said Sabrina, looking down at herself. She now looked just like Ben, and she had his clothes for good measure.

“Yes! Alright!” said Ben, running his fingers through his new blonde hair, then looking back at the outfits he had been admiring. “Okay, I’m gonna go try this dress on.”

Ben took a bright red formal dress from the rack, then headed for the changing rooms. While Sabrina waited, she flexed her arms and noted that even though Ben didn’t do a lot of working out, he was in good shape.

A few moments later, Ben came out wearing the dress, and modeled it for Sabrina. “You look so good in this,” he said, twirling. “I look good in it, I mean.”

“Well, lets buy it and move on,” said Sabrina, eager to get out of here.

“Not yet,” said Ben, going back into the changing room and talking through the door. “We can’t leave without getting at least one pair of shoes.”

Sabrina frowned, and waited until Ben came back out, redressed in Sabrina’s clothes.

“It’ll only take a minute,” said Ben, handing Sabrina the dress and heading for the shoe section.

“Jesus. Is this what I’m usually like?” Sabrina wondered out loud as Ben browsed through the footwear.

“These are nice, right?” Ben asked, trying on a pair of black open-toe heels and walking up and down the aisle.

“Sure.”

“Okay, I know you want to leave, so I’m going to settle on these, but I may come back and exchange them tomorrow.”

“So you are thinking about staying this way longer,” said Sabrina as the pair walked to the register.

“Not permanently, obviously,” said Ben. “But I’m definitely having fun.”

Sabrina smiled as Ben made his purchase, then they headed back out into the open mall.

“Now it’s your turn,” said Ben. “What do you want to do with my personality?”
1532212.title: “Your body, Sabrina.”

153222.content:
The pair approached Ben and Eric’s room and knocked on the door. When there was no answer, they figured Eric must not be there. Ben unlocked the door, and the pair walked in.

“I always forget how small your TV is,” Sabrina mentioned, looking at the television stand in the corner of the room.

“It’s not small,” said Ben. “It’s perfectly normal. Besides, do you have a TV in your room? No.”

“Whatever,” said Sabrina, getting down and popping Mortal Kombat into Ben’s console. “If we did, it would be bigger than this.”

“Well, not all of us have millionaire roommates,” Ben remarked.

As Sabrina played the game, she found that even though she was enjoying herself, she wasn’t good at it. As she lost fight after fight, she began to enjoy herself less and less.

“I don’t understand. I love this game. I’m supposed to be good at it.”

“Just because you have my personality doesn’t mean you have my skills,” said Ben, sitting down next to her and grabbing a controller.

Sabrina’s next fight was against Ben, which he won, easily.

“Dude, that’s not cool,” said Sabrina. “I want to use the remote.”

“I thought you didn’t want to switch back.”

“I don’t. I want your video gaming ability,” Sabrina explained.

“Can we do that?” Ben wondered, getting the remote out and looking at the many buttons.

“I’m sure we can,” said Sabrina. “I want to be good at this game.”

“Well, it would be a small change,” said Ben. “But you have to give up something that I want.”

“Okay. What do you want?”

Ben, giving into the whims of his current personality, wanted Sabrina’s...
153222.title: The dorms

1532222.content:
“I want your hair,” said Ben.

“You want my... hair?”

“Yes. You have such great hair. I’m super jealous.”

“Oh. Well, alright. Go for it.”

*FLASH*

“Oh, it smells nice too,” said Ben, playing with his new blonde locks.

“You know what? You don’t look half bad,” said Sabrina.

“And what about you? You look incredible with short hair. You should totally get a pixie cut when we switch back.“

“If we switch back,” Sabrina insisted again. “Besides, we’re not done here. Gaming skills, remember?”

“Right, right,” Ben said, brushing his hair back and studying the remote’s settings.

*FLASH*

“Okay. See if that worked.”

Sabrina sat down with the controller and began another match, this time winning it effortlessly.

“Awesome,” she said proudly. “We have to see what else that thing can do. Let’s find someone else to mess with.”

“I don’t know, Sabrina.”

“Come on. It’ll be fun. And there’s still plenty of day left to go shopping, or whatever it was you want to do.”

“Well... okay,” said Ben. “For... science, I guess.”

Stepping out into the hall, Ben and Sabrina decided to test the remote on...
1532222.title: Hair

info.brief_description: Yeah, that’s exactly what it is. ***Gift points for good chapters.***
info.description:
The seemingly normal city of [insert city name here] is forever changed when its citizens come across a unique device invented by local scientist Dr. Percy Edwards.

Yes, it’s a body swapping device. You know the sort. A small, handheld remote with all manner of buttons and dials. The remote can swap traits between two or more people, and alters reality as well. How did Dr. Edwards create such a device? Beats me. I’m no scientist.

The remote can swap the following:
Body
Life
Clothing
Personality
All swaps can be complete or partial. For example, two people could swap entire bodies, or they could just swap an individual part like hair or legs. Body parts can be swapped, but not misplaced (i.e. no swapping hands with feet or giving someone extra parts).
The user also has the option of whether or not they want their victims to know what is being swapped.
There is no Reset function on the remote. A complicated mess will require a creative solution.

**MAIN CHARACTERS**
BEN - Benjamin Daniel Hansen - 18m, Caucasian, freshman college student, straight, short brown hair, thin build, Biology major, wants to be a science teacher, Eric’s roommate, nice guy for the most part, has no enemies, drives a hand-me-down 4-door sedan, stays in shape via treadmill/basketball/some weightlifting

SABRINA - Sabrina Green - 19f, Caucasian, freshman college student, straight, medium length blonde hair, average build, Biology major, wants to be a cancer researcher, Maggie’s roommate, has a crush on Ben, flirty and fun-loving but generally more responsible than her friends, assistant manager at a grocery store in the summertime, stays in shape via jogging/volleyball/yoga

ERIC - Eric Little - 19m, African-American, freshman college student, straight, dark brown fade, muscular but not wide, Ben’s roommate, in an unofficial relationship with Simone, die-hard PlayStation fan, stays in shape via mostly weightlifting and basketball

MAGGIE - Margaret Chan - 18f, Chinese-American, freshman college student, bi-curious, long straight black hair, petite build, Business major, Sabrina’s roommate, comes from a rich family, fluent in Mandarin and French, learned German but remembers very little, will answer to “Mags” for close friends, drives a vintage Volkswagen Beetle and a new Tesla Model S, stays in shape via aerobics/yoga

PERCY - Dr. Percy Edwards - 50m, Caucasian, scientist, short gray hair, tall, brilliant, creator of many incredible inventions including the remote

Other character descriptions can be found here:
https://www.writing.com/main/view_item/item_id/2238860-NABSRS-Character-Page

*****GIFT POINTS*****
Don’t be afraid to add chapters. I would love for this story to get as big as possible! I’ll give out 200 gift points each for chapters that aren’t mine, provided they have proper grammar, follow the rules listed below, and follow the chosen story path. I reserve the right to edit or delete chapters that are illegible, too long, out of place, or just plain bad. If you have any questions, feel free to message me.

*****RULES - PLEASE READ*****
The remote does not have any hidden features. Only what is listed above.
The remote only works on humans. No animal stuff.
Feel free to add characters, but check the character page linked above to make sure you aren’t adding anyone redundant.
No killing characters.
I’m pretty lenient on sex scenes, but don’t just throw it in. Make it organic.
I’m happy to edit a mistake or a misspelling here and there, but I WILL delete lazy writing. I’m not your grammar teacher.

Any exceptions to anything posted above must be run by me before being published, otherwise it is subject to deletion without notice.

And most importantly... HAVE FUN!
info.pretty_title: Not Another Body Swapping Remote Story
